name: Jack Six
abstract: Jack Six (July 26, 1930 – March 14, 2015) was an American jazz double-bassist and composer. Six was born in Danville, Illinois, and initially learned trumpet as a teenager before switching to bass.

works.t: Sonata for Piano, Bass, and Drums in D Minor

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: The name 'Jack Six' does not appear in the transcript. The interviewer mentions 'Jack Sheedy', 'Jack Weeks', and 'Jack Fina', but the interviewee (Dave Brubeck) does not mention Jack Six or describe any relationship with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: met a couple of musicians, one of them was a guy I’ve known ever since, a bass player by the name of Jack Six.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Jack Six and those guys made some wonderful records together.
